gaps: Pricing-anchor inconsistency: spec says when concrete prices are given, use $ figures; user gave $400, but Core and Premium use placeholder tokens ($C₁, $C₂) instead of anchored dollar figures
Minor: Depth dependency 'Pivot framing requires Interview-min' is awkwardly worded/truncated
Follow-up menu offers three options instead of one as spec instructs ('offer one based on context')
suggested_fixes: Since user provided $400 anchor and named sub-niche could be inferred, provide $ ranges for Core/Premium tiers rather than tokens, or tighten the preamble to justify why tokens remain
Reword Depth dependency to 'Career-pivot framing requires 45-min interview minimum' for clarity
Trim follow-up menu to a single most-relevant offer (likely the dollar-anchoring option) per spec's 'offer one' rule

gaps: Part 3 flagship-protection proof reveals Starter inversion ($1,450 BYO sum < $2,000 flagship) but ships the ladder as-is with fixes only as suggestions — spec requires flagship-protection rule to hold, not be optionally repaired

Ceiling thresholds for Slots A/B/C ($1,800 / $4,500 / $9,000) sit below the existing tier prices ($2,000 / $5,000 / $10,000), creating a dead zone where BYO routes to a tier before matching its price — should align ceilings to tier prices or above

Vertical Specialization dimension lists '+20% surcharge' as a level, conflating pricing with configuration; surcharges belong in the pricing ladder, levels should be Generalist/Specialist only (which it also states, creating ambiguity)
suggested_fixes: Pick the Foundation floor fix (raise to $950 + adjust ladder) and re-emit the proof table with Starter showing a positive discount, rather than offering it as a follow-up
Set Slot A ceiling at $2,000, Slot B at $5,000, Slot C at $10,000 so routing triggers at parity with the next tier, not below it
Remove '+20% surcharge' from the Levels column in Part 2 and keep it solely as a line item in the BYO pricing ladder

gaps: Part 3 pricing logic for Lite invokes '$300–500 self-paced course benchmarks' — this is a generic category norm (online courses) rather than a named sub-niche like 'enterprise sales course for freelancers', borderline against the Pricing-Anchor Rule's bright line
Premium add-ons line in BYO uses a range ('+$75–150 each') rather than a single price, which a configurator cannot enforce deterministically
Flagship-protection math for Premium includes a vague '$400 (add-ons)' lump that doesn't reconcile with the per-item '+$75–150 each' line above
suggested_fixes: Reframe Lite anchor against the specific sub-niche (e.g., 'priced below comparable freelance-business cohorts like Freelancing School / WorkMade tiers') or convert to placeholder tokens
Replace '+$75–150 each' with a fixed per-add-on price (e.g., '+$125 each') so the configurator has a single value
Itemize the Premium add-on stack in the flagship-protection calc (e.g., 4 add-ons × $125 = $500) so the sum is auditable

gaps: Vague-input preamble misused: memory note format ('Memory unavailable; proceeding without preamble') contradicts spec which says to omit silently when memory is unavailable
Flagship-protection math for Atelier is hand-waved: stated BYO sum is $36K (below $42K flagship) and only reaches $48K via an unstated 'implied volume bump' not in the upgrade table

Add-on pricing inconsistency: Atelier 'includes 1 add-on (~$4K avg)' and House 'includes 2 add-ons (~$8K)' but per-dimension table lists add-ons ranging $3K–$6K with Animatics priced per-piece, making averages ambiguous
suggested_fixes: Remove the 'Memory unavailable' line entirely per spec: 'If memory is unavailable, omit the preamble silently'
Make the Atelier BYO sum explicit using only listed upgrades (e.g., add a Volume: Standard→High +$8K line item) so the $48K total is auditable against the $42K flagship
Specify which add-on is included at each tier (or give a fixed included-add-on credit value) instead of '~$4K avg' to make flagship-protection math reproducible

gaps: Part 3 pricing logic for Clay Open uses 'Free / MIT' which is a valid anchor but skips the Pricing-Anchor Rule's placeholder-token convention inconsistently — Pro and Studio use +$B/+$C tokens while BYO uses +$a..+$m, creating two parallel token namespaces that could confuse readers

Tier names renamed (Clay Open/Pro/Studio) but the Lite/Core/Premium mapping is only shown parenthetically once — spec says 'rename in the brand's vocabulary' which is fine, but Premium tier's 'Custom dev hours quarterly' blurs the line into bespoke consulting (arguably out-of-scope per the service-vs-product spec)

Flagship-protection rule states '~15–25% above' for Pro and '≥30%' for Studio but without concrete numbers this is unverifiable — the rule is asserted, not proven, which the inline example does prove ('$45K naming sum-of-parts')
Closing follow-up menu offers three options (a/b/c) instead of the spec's 'offer one based on context'
suggested_fixes: Unify token namespace: use $A/$B/$C for tiers and $a..$m for BYO atoms with an explicit note that BYO atoms sum toward tier tokens, or collapse to one scheme
Either drop 'Custom dev hours' from Studio (keep it product-shaped) or flag it explicitly as a bounded add-on atom rather than open-ended consulting
Add a worked sum-of-parts line proving Pro flagship beats BYO equivalent (e.g., '$a+$b+$c+$e+$g+$i+$j = 1.2×$B') even with tokens
Replace the three-option closer with a single recommended follow-up (likely pricing validation, given the open $ variables)
